questions.question: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'. Some of these chroniclers were (1)/ mere painstaking men whom (2)/ noted facts and dates with care. (3)/ No error (4)
questions.explanation: The subjective pronoun 'who' should be used in place of the objective 'whom', as it is being used to refer to the subject 'men'. C is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'. The language which Chaucer used (1)/ was English, though still not quite the (2)/ English which we use today. (3)/ No error (4)
questions.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct and error-free. A is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following question, four words are given out of which only one word is correctly spelled. Find the correctly spelled word.
questions.explanation: The correct spellings are - Exuberant, Apartheid, Comeuppance.

questions.question: In the following question, four words are given out of which one word is incorrectly spelt. Find the incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is Dissimilitude.

questions.question: In the below question, a sentence has been given in Direct/Indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Indirect/Direct speech. "Must I go first?" asked Harriet plaintively.
options: Harriet asked plaintively if she might go first.
Harriet asked plaintively if she must go first.
Harriet asked plaintively if she must goes first.
Harriet asked plaintively if she should go first.
questions.explanation: Option B is the right answer. Option A is eliminated as this option seeks to convey that Harriet is asking permission to go first, while the original sentence gives the meaning that Harriet is reluctant to go first. Option C is eliminated as it is grammatically incorrect; the base form of the verb (go) should follow 'must'. Option D is eliminated as it changes the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: In the below question, sentence/passage is split into four parts and named P, Q, R & S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find out which of the four combinations is correct. 1.Relaxed and amused, he turned on to his back and lay listening to the quiet. P. No rain in that sky; only an echo of the peace that held the world in this contented quiet. Q. Through the wide-open window he could see the evening sky, still grey but faintly luminous and streaked with level cloud. R. He savored it, and luxuriated in his long reprieve. S. The immemorial quiet. 6.Oh, well, he would go for a walk, his mind was made up.
questions.explanation: The passage begins with sentence 1 telling us that he lay on his back and listened to the quiet. It is clear that sentence S must follow the sequence after sentence 1, as it establishes continuity with it by giving a further description of the atmosphere. Options C and D are eliminated. Sentences Q and P are a pair: Q gives us the description of the cloudy evening sky, and P follows by giving an account of the weather. Option B is eliminated. Option A is the right answer.

questions.question: In the below question, sentence/passage is split into four parts and named P, Q, R & S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find out which of the four combinations is correct. 1.The books in the library varied from the scientific, through the sentimental, to the purely fantastic. P. From kelp-burning to the saints and heroes. Q. On one thing all were agreed, however: the Islands were magic. R. From bird-watching to soul's pilgrimages. S. They varied, too, from the admirable but dull to the unbelievably bad. 6.The Islands were the last refuge of civilisation in a world gone mad.
questions.explanation: The passage begins with sentence 1 telling us about the variety of books in a library. It is clear that either sentence P or R must follow the sequence after sentence 1, as they establish continuity with it. Since there is no option beginning with R, we can be certain that P will follow sentence 1. Options C and D are eliminated. Sentence Q ends the sequence, as it establishes continuity with Sentence 6; both sentences talk about the magic of the Islands. Option A is eliminated. Option B is the right answer.

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentences and mark it. Rule by the wealthy
questions.explanation: 'Autocracy' is another word for dictatorship. 'Democracy' is rule by the common people. 'Anarchy' is a state of disorder or no rule. 'Plutocracy' refers to government/ rule by the wealthy.

questions.question: Sentences given with blanks to be filled in with an appropriate word(s). Four alternatives are suggested for each question. Choose the correct alternative out of the four. _________ on curbs, the emphasis has to be on constructive policy.
questions.explanation: The words 'although' and 'however' are used to emphasize the contrast between two things (contrastive conjunctions). Here, the context is 'preference' and not 'contrast'. Hence, 'rather than' is the appropriate choice.

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the idiom/phrase in bold. Choose the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. To play fast and loose
questions.explanation: The idiom means to be irresponsible

questions.question: Each of the following questions containing a word in bold letters followed by four options. Select the option that is most similar in meaning to the main word in bold letters. Barbaric
questions.explanation: Barbaric means cruel or brutal.

questions.question: A sentence is given with a part of it in brackets. Given below are the alternatives to the bracketed part A), B), C) which may improve the sentence, choose the correct alternative. In case no improvement is needed, your answer is D). Mr. Kulkarni is planning to settle in Kanpur as soon as he (will retire) in June next year.
questions.explanation: The correct usage is '..as soon as he retires..'

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentences. To make deep cuts
questions.explanation: Inculpate means to accuse. Infuriate means to enrage. Expurgate means to remove and lacerate means to make deep cuts.

questions.question: (i)=?
questions.explanation: Dare means to have the courage to do something. Deem means to regard or consider in a special way. Drain means to dry out something by allowing the liquid in it to flow out completely. Doom means terrible fates like death, destruction etc. So the correct answer is 'doomed'. When a man is overtaken by adversity he feels as if he was doomed forever. This means that when some terrible fate happens in a man's life, he thinks that he is destroyed for ever.

questions.question: (ii)=?
questions.explanation: Affect: To cause a change in something. Effect: To bring about, to result in something. Flame: To burn Impact: To strike with a blow The most suitable word is Impact. "Many a weak mind has committed suicide because they found the impact of misfortune unbearable". Impact of misfortune means "blow of misfortune" which is more suitable to the context than effect of misfortune which means "result of misfortune" So the correct option is C.

questions.question: (iii)=?
questions.explanation: Resist means to prevent the action of something. Revert means to return to a previous state. Persist means to continue to exist in spite of the difficulties. Reincarnation means the rebirth of the soul in another body. The word 'persevered' gives us a hint. So the correct answer is 'persisted'.

questions.question: (iv)=?
questions.explanation: Attenuate means to reduce in thickness, to become thin. Erase means to rub out. Lift means to pick up and move to a different position, or to rise. The most suitable answer is 'lift'. Clouds will not run, attenuate or erase.

questions.question: (v)=?
questions.explanation: Greet means to welcome. Treat means to serve or deal with in a certain way. Lift means to pick up and move to a different position. Hence the most suitable word is 'greeted' as the rising sun cannot be treated, seated or lifted.

questions.question: Fill in the blanks It is to be hoped that we shall often have the ___________ to witness this interesting work; for as a rule great musical compositions are peculiarly unfortunate among works of art, in being known at first hand by comparatively few persons.
questions.explanation: The word 'chance' would fit in the sentence, but will not be the best fit as it implies a possibility of something happening. Option C is eliminated, as the word 'avenue', means a way of approaching a problem, while the passage needs a word meaning 'a suitable occasion'. Option D is eliminated, as the word 'prospect', which means likelihood, does not exactly fit in the context of the sentence. Option A is the right answer, as the word 'opportunity' means a suitable occasion, which fits accurately in the sentence.

questions.question: Out of the five alternatives, choose the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. APPOSITE
questions.explanation: Apposite means suited or appropriate to the purpose. Example: He doesn't know which clothes are apposite for the ceremony.

questions.question: Out of the five alternatives, choose the one which is opposite in meaning to the given word. SAGACIOUS
questions.explanation: Sagacious means wise, intelligent, good judgement etc. Ruthless means lacking compassion for others. So the antonym from among the given alternatives is Silly. Example: My teachers still remember that I was a very sagacious student in my school days

questions.question: Choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word. Dabbler
questions.explanation: Dabbler means a person who regularly or occasionally engages in an activity as a pastime rather than as a profession. Hence, the word opposite in meaning is Professional.

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the idiom/phrase in bold. Choose the correct alternative that best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. Above the board
questions.explanation: Above the board means open and honest or transparent.

questions.question: In each of the questions, a part of the sentence is in brackets that may contain an error. Four alternatives are given for the bracketed portion. Identify the one which replaces the bracketed part to form a logically and grammatically correct statement. The rampant dumping of plastic (in the sewerage at) Maharashtra has caused pollution hazards.
questions.explanation: 'Dumping' shows motion, hence 'into' will be used at the start of the phrase. So, options A and C can be eliminated. Between B and D; D uses the preposition 'of' which is incorrect. Hence option B is the correct answer.

questions.question: A sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active voice We do not sell any products that are adulterated.
options: Adulterated products were not sold by us.
Adulterated products were not being sold by us.
Adulterated products are not sold by us
Adulterated products are not being sold by us.
questions.explanation: The construction 'are not sold' should be used here, as the present tense of the sentence is to be kept intact. C is the right answer.
